rubric.target_patient: Adult women (18+, including a dedicated elderly subgroup ≥70) newly diagnosed with unilateral, histologically confirmed primary invasive breast carcinoma at an early stage, who are candidates for biomarker-guided induction therapy (endocrine, neoadjuvant chemotherapy, or targeted therapy) as part of a personalized, risk-adapted treatment strategy rather than metastatic or recurrent disease management.
rubric.site_burden: No schedule of assessments was provided in the record, so burden is inferred rather than read from protocol detail. The design implies: (1) upfront biomarker-based risk/response assessment (e.g., Ki-67 or equivalent) driving assignment to one of several sub-protocols (endocrine, chemo, targeted), requiring coordinated pathology/biomarker sample handling; (2) management of patients across multiple concurrent treatment arms with distinct monitoring needs; (3) an 8-year outcome horizon implying sustained longitudinal follow-up infrastructure; and (4) very high per-site enrollment load, since 4936 patients were enrolled across only 2 sites (~2400 patients/site), implying a large, high-throughput breast cancer practice or academic breast center rather than a low-volume community site.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must specialize in breast cancer diagnosis and multi-modality treatment (surgical oncology, medical oncology, or gynecologic oncology with a breast focus) to appropriately manage assignment across endocrine, chemotherapy, and targeted-therapy sub-protocols.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists hematology/oncology, medical oncology, surgical oncology, or gynecologic oncology, AND the physician practices within a designated breast center/program.
criteria.derived_from: Intervention description: sub-protocols including endocrine therapy, neoadjuvant chemotherapy, or targeted therapy; site footprint shows both sites are dedicated Brustzentren (breast centers).

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have demonstrated expertise in biomarker-based risk stratification and response prediction in breast cancer (e.g., Ki-67, genomic recurrence scores) consistent with the trial's core scientific aim.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed record includes authored/co-authored publications on breast cancer biomarkers, risk assessment, or therapy response prediction (e.g., Ki-67, recurrence score).
criteria.derived_from: Primary outcome: identification of a responder subpopulation with outcome comparable to HR+/RS≤11 using biomarker-driven risk assessment.

criteria.requirement: The practice must see a high volume of newly diagnosed early-stage invasive breast cancer patients spanning adult and elderly age ranges, sufficient to support the trial's very high per-site enrollment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Medicare claims show breast cancer diagnostic/treatment billing (biopsy, mastectomy, chemo administration) at or above comparable breast-center benchmarks, OR self-report confirms an annual new breast cancer diagnosis volume adequate for large-scale trial enrollment.
criteria.derived_from: Enrollment of 4936 patients across only 2 sites; inclusion of age 18+ with a distinct ADAPT Elderly (≥70) subgroup.

criteria.requirement: Practice must regularly diagnose and treat female patients with unilateral, primary (non-recurrent, non-metastatic) invasive breast carcinoma at the point of initial treatment decision-making.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms that the majority of the physician's breast cancer caseload consists of newly diagnosed, early-stage, unilateral disease rather than recurrent/metastatic referrals.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion criteria: female patients with histologically confirmed unilateral primary invasive carcinoma of the breast.

criteria.requirement: Site must function as, or be embedded within, a multidisciplinary certified breast center capable of coordinating surgery, chemotherapy, endocrine therapy, and targeted therapy under a single induction-therapy decision pathway.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site is a named/certified Brustzentrum or equivalent multidisciplinary breast center, confirmed by site listing or self-report describing integrated surgical/medical/radiation oncology services.
criteria.derived_from: Both listed sites (LMU Breast Center, Ev. Krankenhaus Bethesda Brustzentrum Niederrhein) are designated breast centers.

criteria.requirement: Site must maintain infrastructure for sustained patient follow-up and outcome tracking over an 8-year horizon as required by the primary endpoint.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms an established longitudinal tumor registry/follow-up program capable of tracking breast cancer patients for 8+ years.
criteria.derived_from: Primary outcome timeframe of 8 years for identifying a responder subpopulation.

criteria.requirement: Site must have tissue/biomarker sample collection and shipping workflows to support centralized biomarker assessment used to stratify induction therapy assignment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms an established pathology workflow for biopsy/tissue processing and shipment to a reference lab for biomarker testing.
criteria.derived_from: Brief summary: use of biomarkers in advance of therapy decision-making to personalize treatment.

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to manage patients across multiple concurrent treatment sub-protocols (endocrine, chemotherapy, targeted therapy) with distinct monitoring pathways within a single trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms operational capability to run three or more concurrent treatment arms/sub-studies simultaneously.
criteria.derived_from: Intervention description: therapy dependent on respective sub-protocol, spanning endocrine, chemo, and targeted therapy.

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience serving as a site investigator within academic cooperative group breast cancer trials, consistent with sponsorship by an academic study group.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as an investigator/site contact on at least one completed breast cancer trial sponsored by an academic cooperative group in ClinicalTrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: West German Study Group (OTHER), an academic cooperative research group.

criteria.requirement: Site should demonstrate a track record of contributing large patient numbers to prior trials, consistent with this trial's extremely high per-site enrollment.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov records show the site/investigator contributing enrollment in the hundreds or more on a prior trial, or self-report confirms comparable historical enrollment capacity.
criteria.derived_from: 4936 total enrollment concentrated across only 2 sites (~2400 patients per site).

criteria.requirement: Investigator should show an active pattern of clinical research engagement in breast oncology, evidencing ongoing trial participation beyond routine clinical practice.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research-related payments associated with breast cancer therapeutics tied to the physician or their institution.
criteria.derived_from: Phase 2/3 multi-sub-protocol design sponsored by an academic cooperative group, implying active trial research infrastructure at participating sites.

excluded_screening: ECOG <=1 or KI >=80% performance status — a per-patient eligibility check at enrollment, not a property of the site or physician.
Negative pregnancy test within 7 days prior to induction treatment — per-patient screening logistics, unrelated to physician/site fit.
Written informed consent documentation — a per-patient procedural requirement of trial conduct, not a site qualification.
Exclusion for concurrent participation in another interventional trial within 30 days — a per-patient eligibility/washout rule.
Exclusion for concurrent pregnancy and requirement for non-hormonal contraception — per-patient eligibility criteria.
Exclusion for breastfeeding — a per-patient eligibility criterion with no bearing on investigator suitability.
